workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Samantha Okafor at Evergreen Solutions just sent this. Help me prepare the forecast.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: competitive positioning Discussion - Evergreen Solutions
Date: 2024-01-22
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions), David Chen (Microsoft), Priya (Evergreen Solutions)

[00:02:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on competitive positioning from our last session.

[00:06:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:10:00] Priya (Evergreen Solutions): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:13:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:18:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:21:00] Priya (Evergreen Solutions): On the competitive positioning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-I trap (Misattributes view to wrong team member); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-I failure mode (Misattributes view to wrong team member)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-I -- Misattributes view to wrong team member
